title: HockeyTech SOCKS5 proxy for config extraction
description: Opt-in SOCKS5/HTTP proxy support for HockeyTech config extraction. Branch test/hockeyTechProxy, commits 8f83b02 (expand proxy support) + 49ed73e (add opt-in SOCKS5 proxy). Not yet merged to main/dev.
close_reason: Re-implemented HockeyTech SOCKS5 proxy support on dev from scratch (orphaned branch test/hockeyTechProxy had unmergeable conflicts). Same params/env toggles. Tests: 34/36 pass; the 2 WHL failures are pre-existing on clean dev.

title: Add host-agnostic standard-update.hook.sh deploy hook using /info polling
description: Post-deploy notification hook that reads running git state from the app's /info endpoint (no docker inspect / no bash) and sends a deployment-success repository_dispatch consumed by deployment-webhook.yml. Baked into the app image at /deploy/standard-update.hook.sh (Dockerfile) and run via 'docker exec game-thumbs sh /deploy/standard-update.hook.sh' in the Komodo Post Deploy step. Replaces the Unraid-specific hook/doc (removed). Docs: standard-deployment.md.
notes: Dispatch failed inside container with curl error 77 (HTTP 000): node:lts-slim has curl but not the ca-certificates package, so HTTPS to api.github.com had no CA bundle (/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt missing). App's own HTTPS works because Node bundles CAs; HEALTHCHECK works because it's plain HTTP. Fix: add ca-certificates to the Dockerfile apt install. Requires image rebuild.
close_reason: Added standard-update.hook.sh (POSIX, /info-polling, docker-free) + standard-deployment.md; syntax + live parse verified

title: Wire pixel-regression snapshot harness into CI (.github/workflows)
description: The snapshot harness (test/snapshots/snapshot.js, npm run snapshot) added in game-thumbs-xev runs only manually. Add it to automated testing so pixel regressions are caught in CI. Note: it is NOT a test/*.test.js file so the matrix in .github/workflows/automated-testing.yml does not pick it up, and it compares against a committed manifest.json whose hashes depend on upstream CDN logo bytes that can drift, so a naive 'npm run snapshot' gate may be flaky. Decide handling (dedicated job, allow-failure/report-only, or pin/cache source logos) and integrate accordingly.
close_reason: Added base-vs-head pixel-regression CI job (.github/workflows/pixel-regression.yml) + snapshot --soft mode; verified locally (clean pass, regression fail, soft-error pass). CI-green confirmation will land on the next PR.

title: Fix WHL HockeyTech provider failures (Winterhawks, Thunderbirds return 400)
description: providers.test.js fails 2/36 cases: WHL teams Portland Winterhawks and Seattle Thunderbirds return HTTP 400 instead of 200, and report no provider data (logos/colors). Confirmed pre-existing (fails on unmodified tree), surfaced while verifying game-thumbs-7t5. Likely a HockeyTech provider resolution or request-validation issue for these WHL teams. Investigate why the route 400s, restore provider data so both render 200, and get providers.test.js to 36/36.
close_reason: Root cause: shared chl.ca portal scrape returned wrong/empty HockeyTech client_code, poisoning WHL config+teams cache with OHL data. Fixed by pinning explicit clientCodes (whl/ohl/lhjmq) in leagues.json and relaxing getLeagueConfigAsync to accept clientCode-only configs (apiKey falls back to provider default). providers.test.js 36/36.

title: Shadow helpers + named SHADOWS profiles
description: Replace 15+ inline ctx.shadow* blocks in generators/* and imageUtils.js with setShadow(ctx,profile)/resetShadow(ctx) and a named SHADOWS map. Map each call site 1:1 to a named profile; do NOT normalize differing values. Pixel-preserving.
close_reason: Extracted helpers/shadows.js with named SHADOWS profiles + setShadow/resetShadow; replaced all inline ctx.shadow* blocks across logoGenerator, thumbnailGenerator, genericImageGenerator, imageUtils. 43/43 snapshot cases pixel-identical; test failures (2 WHL) pre-existing.

title: Unify matchup route handlers (thumb/cover/logo)
description: routes/thumb.js, cover.js, logo.js share the same 3-case structure (league/single-team/matchup, badge caching, winner effect). Extract a factory in helpers/routeUtils.js parameterized by generator fn + dimensions. ~500 line win. Reuse resolveTeamsWithFallback, applyWinnerEffect, addBadgeOverlay, sendCachedOrGenerate, handleImageRouteError. Pixel-preserving; verify with harness.
close_reason: Unified thumb/cover/logo via createImageRoute factory + shared renderMatchup tail in helpers/routeUtils.js. Route files 727->242 LOC. Verified pixel-identical (43/43 snapshot cases), full test suite passes, live spot-checks OK.

title: Image regression snapshot/diff harness
description: Add a script + npm task that renders a fixed matrix of representative requests (each generator style; league/team/matchup; badge; winner; overlays) to PNGs and diffs against a committed baseline built from main. Gates all pixel-preserving refactors. Verify: run on unchanged code yields zero diffs.
close_reason: Snapshot regression harness added: test/snapshots/snapshot.js + README, npm run snapshot/snapshot:update, committed manifest.json (43 cases). Verified update+check both clean, 43/43 pixel-identical on no-op re-run.

title: Cleanup epic: dedup, maintainability & docs
description: Large-scale code cleanup, deduplication, maintainability, and docs cleanup across game-thumbs. Hard constraint: image output must stay pixel-identical (intentionally-differing generator constants preserved exactly). Children grouped: regression harness (prereq), code dedup+helpers, dead code+tests, large-file splits, docs cleanup, plus a league-route badge feature. Executed one task at a time.

title: Document boxing support and TheSportsDB athlete provider
description: Add user + technical docs for the new boxing league and TheSportsDBAthleteProvider: supported-leagues.md (Boxing section under Combat Sports, no feature flag; provider config example), technical-details.md (provider subsection: free-key search-by-name, sport guard, ~30-day data/link cache, no image caching). Relates to game-thumbs-ont.
close_reason: Documented boxing: supported-leagues.md (Boxing section under Combat Sports, no-flag note, examples, name-matching caveat, TheSportsDB Athlete provider config example), technical-details.md (TheSportsDB Athlete Provider subsection), README supports line.

title: Add boxing support via TheSportsDB athlete provider
description: ESPN does not carry boxing (Invalid sport). Add a reusable TheSportsDBAthleteProvider using free-key searchplayers.php (sport-guarded), with aggressive ~30-day data/link caching (not image assets). Wire boxing league (TSDB id 4445) gated by LEAGUES_ENABLE_BOXING.
close_reason: Added TheSportsDBAthleteProvider (free-key search-by-name, sport-guarded, ~30d data/link cache, no image caching) and boxing league (TSDB 4445, LEAGUES_ENABLE_BOXING). Verified Canelo/Fury resolve, Garcia rejected, league badge resolves, and a matchup thumbnail renders.

title: Regular-weight custom subtitle/title fonts silently fall back to default
description: Custom league fonts (titleFont/subtitleFont) only render when the font file is Bold/Italic. A Regular-weight font is registered but stored with an empty style-flags string in fontRegistry. drawContents() in generators/genericImageGenerator.js selects the custom family via a truthiness check on that flags string, so an empty string (Regular weight) is read as 'not registered' and falls back to default_title/default_subtitle. Fix: use an own-property existence check instead of truthiness. Also fixes a swapped-args call to drawContents in the error fallback path (line ~261).
close_reason: Use own-property existence check instead of truthiness for custom font selection in drawContents; empty style-flags string for Regular-weight fonts no longer reads as unregistered. Also fixed swapped league/title args in error-fallback drawContents call.

title: Fix NRL league-logo 429 (Wikimedia rate limit)
description: GET /nrl/.../thumb.png?style=1&logo=true 429s because NRL has no local logoUrl and falls back to a Wikimedia SVG that gets rate limited. Fix: add local assets/NRL.png + leagues.json logoUrl, cache rasterized SVG league logos on disk via fsCache, and use a descriptive User-Agent for SVG fetches.
notes: Fixed by bundling all 11 remaining Wikimedia league logos as local ./assets/*.png and repointing leagues.json. SVGs pre-rasterized via svgUtils.rasterizeLogo. Also hardened downloadImageWithSvgSupport with a full browser User-Agent for any remaining provider-supplied remote SVGs. Verified: NRL/top14/super-rugby render with centered league logo, no 429/SVG warnings, 463 league tests + error tests pass.
close_reason: Bundled remote league logos as static assets; no more Wikimedia fetch for them

description:
Adds Canadian Baseball League support via a new Supabase provider, expands rugby coverage with 10 new leagues and ESPN data for 7 existing ones, renames two rugby league keys for clarity, and fixes consecutive-slash URL normalization and SVG logo rendering for team covers.

description:

title: v1.8.0 Release Candidate - Generic event support, new soccer leagues, font registry, disk caching
description:
Adds support for generating covers and thumbnails for non-matchup league events (e.g. races) via new `title`, `subtitle`, and `iconurl` query parameters, along with a font registry and bundled Saira Stencil fonts. Includes two new soccer leagues (US Open Cup, USL Championship), an `ALLOW_EVENT_OVERLAYS` environment flag, and a switch from in-memory to filesystem-backed image caching.

Merged 2026-04-27 via PR #120 (sethwv/game-thumbs).

description:

The Docker workflow is now split into distinct jobs for metadata preparation, multi-platform builds, and manifest creation, improving maintainability and reliability. Documentation is updated to reflect the new leagues, providers, and fallback rendering options.

**Docker Build Workflow Improvements:**
- Refactored `.github/workflows/build-docker.yml` to use a three-stage job

title: Refactor caching to use filesystem and improve provider error handling
description:
This pull request introduces significant improvements to caching and error handling across the codebase, primarily by replacing in-memory caches with filesystem-backed caches, enhancing cache reliability and scalability. It also adds a shared error class for better error reporting, and makes several adjustments to Docker build workflow and request configuration usage.

**Caching Improvements (Filesystem-backed):**

* Added a new `fsCache.js` utility to provide filesystem-backed caching for buffers and JSON, replacing in-memory Maps for processed images and badge overlays. This enables pers
...

title: Add winner highlight, 3D embossed style 98, root docs redirect, and expanded league support
description:
This pull request introduces a new "winner" effect to the API, allowing users to highlight the winning team in matchup images by displaying the losing team in greyscale. This feature is fully documented and implemented across the logo, cover, and thumb endpoints. Additionally, there are improvements to Docker configuration, including an option to redirect the root path to documentation, and a new 3D embossed style with league logo support. Below are the most important changes:

**Winner Effect Feature:**

* Added a `winner` query parameter to the `/logo`, `/cover`, and `/thumb` endpoints. 
...

title: FlagCDNProvider: prevent weak country matches
description:
This pull request introduces stricter validation and improved matching logic to the `FlagCDNProvider` class, making team identification more robust and less error-prone. The main changes focus on input validation and ensuring only strong matches are accepted when resolving country/team names.

Input validation improvements:

* Rejects empty or whitespace-only `teamIdentifier` inputs and throws a `TeamNotFoundError` if encountered.
* Rejects normalized inputs that are shorter than two characters to prevent weak or ambiguous matches.

Matching logic enhancements:

* Sets a minimum match
...

title: Improve Country Matching
description:
This pull request enhances the country name matching logic in the `FlagCDNProvider` to improve user input flexibility, especially for cases where users omit spaces in country names. The main changes focus on improving how input strings are compared to country names by handling space-insensitive matches.

Improvements to matching logic:

* Added a check to treat input that matches a country name with spaces removed (e.g., "unitedstates" matching "united states") as a high-confidence match, scoring it between an exact match and a starts-with match.
* Added a check to handle cases where the 
...

The documentation is expanded to explain the structure, required fields, use cases, and resolution priority for custom teams.

title: Handle unsupported image formats and parallelize team resolution
description:

- Parallelize team resolution to resolve both teams simultaneously
- Parallelize feeder league checks using Promise.all()
- Optimize fallback generation: when both teams fail, download and process league logo once instead of twice
- Improve error handling in generateFallbackTeamObject() to preserve greyed league logo fallback behavior

Fixes "Unsupported image type" errors when league logos return SVG or invalid formats. Significantly improves performa

description:

It also improves team logo resolution fallback logic and updates documentation to reflect expanded league support and new features. The most important changes are grouped below.

### Badge Overlay Feature

title: Repo Updates & Improved Fallback
description:
This pull request introduces a more robust and visually consistent fallback mechanism for API endpoints that generate team-based images (cover, logo, thumb). When a requested team is not found and fallback is enabled, the API now uses a greyscale league logo with light grey colors as a placeholder for missing teams in matchup endpoints, instead of simply returning the league image. This change is reflected in both the backend logic and the documentation, ensuring that missing teams are handled gracefully and clearly indicated in generated images.

title: [Feature]: Team Broadcast Indicator
description: ### Problem or Use Case When creating images for baseball games with a source that includes home/away broadcasts; you do not have a visual differentiation between the two images. ### Proposed Solution Add a query parameter that allows a symbol or other visual aid to represent that the generated matchup logo is for a specific team of the two displayed. Below is a example of how this could look. I'm not happy with this particular visual solution; but I'm struggling to think of something that could work across all logos and styles. Maybe something more noticeable and less ugly than a yellow circle. I think the 'winner' matchup style is more in the right direction. ### Feature Category Image generation enhancement ### League Details (if requesting new league) _No response_ ### Team Data Override (if correcting team data) _No response_ ### Examples or Mockups https://gamethumbs.local/mlb/NewYorkYankees/BostonRedSox/thumb.png?broadcast=1&style=1 ### Additional Context _No response_

title: [Feature]: Generic NCAA slug for multi-sport college channels
description:
### Problem or Use Case

ESPN+ and similar services carry multiple NCAA sports on the same channel group (basketball, softball, baseball, lacrosse). Currently each sport
needs a specific slug (ncaam, ncaabb, ncaasbw, etc.) which requires sport detection on the client side.  

Request:

A generic ncaa slug that generates team composites with neutral NCAA branding (no sport-specific overlay). This would allow IPTV managers to assign composite logos to college matchup channels without needing to detect the specific sport.

Use case:

~140 active ESPN+ matchup channels daily, rotating across multiple NCAA sports. Team names are parseable from channel names (e.g., "Marquette vs Arizona State") but the sport is not reliably detectable from the name alone — "Howard vs Columbia" could be basketball, softball, or lacrosse.

Currently we'd need to guess between ncaam, ncaaw, ncaabb, ncaasbw, etc., and wrong guesses produce misleading sport-specific branding on the composite.

A neutral ncaa slug would let us use one slug for all college matchups regardless of sport.


### Proposed Solution

 Add ncaa as a valid league slug that uses a generic NCAA logo (e.g., the NCAA wordmark) instead of a sport-specific one. The composite generation
  logic stays identical — just a new entry in the league config mapping ncaa to a neutral logo asset.

  Pseudocode (based on how existing slugs appear to work):

  "ncaa": {
      "name": "NCAA",
      "logo": "ncaa_logo.png",    // generic NCAA wordmark, no sport icon
      "source": "espn"            // or whatever team data source covers college teams
  }

  The team matching already works — ncaam, ncaaw, ncaabb, ncaasbw all resolve the same school names correctly. The only difference between slugs is
  the league branding overlay. A generic ncaa slug would use the same team resolution but with a sport-neutral logo.

  This keeps the existing sport-specific slugs for users who know the sport, while giving a safe fallback for multi-sport channel groups where the
  sport can't be determined from the channel name alone.

### Feature Category

Image generation enhancement

### League Details (if requesting new league)

_No response_

### Team Data Override (if correcting team data)

_No response_

### Examples or Mockups

_No response_

### Additional Context

_No response_

title: [Bug]: No NCAA Football Style 6 Matchup
description:
### Bug Description

When I call `/ncaa/football/georgia/alabama/logo?style=6&logo=true` it returns an unsupported image error. 

However, other NCAA sports seem to work: `/ncaa/basketball/georgia/alabama/logo?style=6&logo=true`

### Bug Type

Endpoint/API issue (wrong image, error response, etc.)

### Affected Endpoint (if applicable)

/ncaa/football/georgia/alabama/logo?style=6&logo=true

### League/Sport (if applicable)

NCAAF

### Error Logs or Screenshots

_No response_

title: [Feature]: TV Logo Badges
description:
### Problem or Use Case

I really love the little badges for what network a matchup is on, what if we incorporated channel logos instead of just the little "NBC" badge.

### Proposed Solution

I'd like the API to store TV station logos and use those for the badges to let you know what channel a matchup is on, instead of the generic black text on white background.

### Feature Category

New image style/layout

title: [Feature]: Fallback League Logo
description:
### Problem or Use Case

When there is no logo available in Emby it just says the channel name, if a default logo could be populated I would think it would fix that issue. Just live a TV Icon or something simple.

### Proposed Solution

Maybe something that's configurable on the user end?
Like an environment variable that sets a backup url/file path?

This league image would be used if fallback=true on the endpoints that support it, when a league logo is not available from the provider for generation as a fallback cover/thumb/logo, or as the league logo placed on other generated images.

### Feature Category

Image generation enhancement

title: Badge param on league-level routes
description: Add badge= support to /:league/cover, /:league/thumb, and /:league/logo?style=1 (badges currently apply only to matchup routes). Relates to TV Logo Badges bead. Leave OPEN for now, do not start.

title: Fill in CLAUDE.md placeholders
description: Fill the Build & Test, Architecture Overview, and Conventions & Patterns placeholders in CLAUDE.md with real content.

title: Document missing env vars / config hub
description: Add ~10 undocumented vars to docs/docker.md (THESPORTSDB_API_KEY, HOCKEYTECH_API_KEY, HOCKEYTECH_PROXY_*, CBL_SUPABASE_API_KEY, APP_MODE, XC_PROXY, SCRAPER_USER_AGENT, SOCKS_PROXY, LOG_DIR, LOG_ROTATION_SIZE). Make docker.md the single config hub.

title: Decompose large generator functions
description: Break each large style function in logoGenerator.js/thumbnailGenerator.js into drawBackground/drawLogos/drawLeagueLogo/drawBadges helpers. Structure only, output unchanged. Depends on Group A.

title: Split imageUtils.js (1520 LOC)
description: Separate into focused modules: core canvas/image ops, logo selection/fallback (team resolution + selectBestLogo), badge overlay. Keep a thin re-export shim so importers don't all change at once. Pixel-preserving. Depends on Group A.
close_reason: Split imageUtils.js (1539 LOC) into helpers/image/{draw,imageIO,logoResolution,badge}.js behind a thin re-export shim (78 LOC) preserving the exact 26-name API. Pure code move; verified pixel-identical (43/43) and full test suite green.

title: Resolve .DISABLED test files
description: test/ncaa.test.js.DISABLED and test/performance.test.js.DISABLED: repair+re-enable or delete with a note, decided per file.

title: Remove dead functions (drawLogoWithOutline, getWhiteLogo)
description: Both unused outside imageUtils.js (verified via grep). Remove both + the export. Keep drawLogoMaintainAspect (it IS used). Verify npm test green and no remaining refs.

title: League-logo fetch helper
description: Wrap the repeated getLeagueLogoUrl(leagueObj,false/true) pair-fetching used across routes into a single helper.

title: applyBadgeWithCaching helper
description: Extract the badge validate->base-cache->generate-or-reuse->overlay pattern duplicated across the 3 routes. Folds into A1 (route unification).
close_reason: Superseded by game-thumbs-htw: the badge validate->base-cache->generate-or-reuse->overlay pattern was extracted as renderMatchup in helpers/routeUtils.js during the route unification.

title: Named layout/dimension constants module
description: New config/constants.js holding canvas dimensions, logo-size multipliers, and the intentionally-differing diagonal-split percentages, each named per generator. Pure rename of literals; NO value changes.

title: calculateCenteredDimensions helper
description: Extract the repeated aspect-ratio centering block in logoGenerator.js/thumbnailGenerator.js into calculateCenteredDimensions(containerSize, aspectRatio) in imageUtils.js. Pixel-preserving.
close_reason: Added loadProcessedLogo + calculateCenteredDimensions to helpers/imageUtils.js; replaced ~24 download/trim/loadImage triples and ~20 aspect-centering blocks across the 3 generators. Verified pixel-identical (43/43 snapshot cases) and full test suite green.

title: loadProcessedLogo helper
description: Collapse the download->trim->loadImage triple repeated 20+ times in the generators into loadProcessedLogo(url, trim=true). Pixel-preserving.
close_reason: Added loadProcessedLogo + calculateCenteredDimensions to helpers/imageUtils.js; replaced ~24 download/trim/loadImage triples and ~20 aspect-centering blocks across the 3 generators. Verified pixel-identical (43/43 snapshot cases) and full test suite green.

title: Dedupe duplicate league keys in leagues.json (nrl, top14, super-rugby)
description: leagues.json defines nrl, top14, and super-rugby twice each (around lines 2029-2058 and 2290-2421). JSON.parse silently keeps the last occurrence, so the earlier definitions are dead config and an easy source of drift/bugs. Reconcile each pair into a single entry and remove the shadowed copies. Latent tech debt, not currently breaking anything.

title: De-dup and trim docs prose
description: Resolve team-matching vs customization overlap (cross-reference, don't repeat); trim the ~15-20% bloat in docker.md/customization.md.

title: Remove docs/contributing.md stub
description: Delete the 16-line docs/contributing.md stub; keep root CONTRIBUTING.md as single source of truth (update docs nav if needed).

title: Consolidate deprecated api-reference stubs
description: Consolidate the 9 redirect stub files in docs/api-reference/ into one page or a deprecated/ folder.

title: Strip confusing dead comments
description: Remove confusing dead/commented code e.g. transparent-stroke 'hidden but can be restored' lines in thumbnailGenerator.js.
